name: Marinated Beef Province Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat into 2-inch cubes.
2. Dice the bacon.
3. Skin and crush the garlic cloves using a firm knife or garlic press with a pinch of salt on the chopping board.
4. Blend the crushed garlic with chopped parsley, ground cloves, and allspice.
5. Place the meat in a dish and add the wine vinegar, red wine, and garlic spice mixture.
6. Peel and thinly slice one third of the onions and carrots. Add them to the marinade along with a little salt and pepper. Allow to marinate for at least 12 hours, turning occasionally.
7. Peel the remaining onions and carrots. Quarter the onions but leave the carrots whole.
8. Heat oil in a large heavy skillet. Fry the diced bacon and quartered onions until golden brown and transfer them into a casserole dish.
9. Remove the meat from the marinade and drain well. Brown the meat in the remaining oil in the pan, then add it to the onions.
10. Pour the marinade liquid and vegetables into the skillet and stir well to absorb the remaining oil.
11. Add the whole carrots, brandy, thyme, parsley sprig, rosemary, zest of the orange, and a small amount of orange juice. Season with salt and pepper.
12. Transfer the mixture over the meat and onions in the casserole. Cover and cook in the center of a slow oven set to 350°F for 2 1/4 hours.
13. To serve, remove the garni (parsley bundle). This dish is particularly good when served with spaghetti, macaroni, or noodles, topped with butter, grated cheese, and a little chopped parsley.
